title: oxytropis leucantha var. hudsonica
intro: Oxytropis leucantha var. hudsonica, also known as white locoweed, is a perennial legume native to the western regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with white locoweed include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. White locoweed can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
